measured.transcript: we will hear argument this morning in case twenty two seventy four sixty six glossop versus oklahoma mr waxman mr chief justice and may it please the court richard glossop was convicted on the word of one man justin sneed the undisputed murderer in this case oklahoma has now disclosed evidence revealing that mr snee lied to the jury about his history of psychiatric treatment including the fact that a prison psychiatry prescribe lithium to treat his previously undiagnosed bipolar disorder the prosecution suppressed that evidence and then failed to correct mr sneed's perjured denial just as it suppressed evidence that in the middle of trial in violation of the court sequestration order sneed altered his testimony about the knife wounds on the victim at the urgent request of the prosecutor who then falsely denied to the court her prior knowledge there is no adequate or independent jurisdictional bar to review and no warrant for an evidentiary hearing as to independence the court's opinion is suffused with merits determination on the brady and and nap who claims and certainly there is no quote clear and express statement that the court's decision is based on a bona fide fide a separate adequate and independent grounds as long required by this court to preclude review nor is there any adequate bar by rejecting the state's waiver the court created a jurisdictional threshold it had never applied in any other case the disposition was quote without support in prior state law as required by over sixty years of this court's precedent to establish adequacy no evidentiary hearing could alter the conclusion that mr glossop was denied due process there's no dispute that contrary to to sneed sworn testimony the state's own suppressed record shows that he was in fact treated by a psychiatrist for bipolar disorder just as there is no dispute that sneed changed his testimony about the knife at the urgent mid trial request of the prosecutor who then falsely denied that very fact to the court this court should reverse and remain for a new trial i welcome the court's questions mr waxman you place quite a bit of weight on the note notes from smother and actually and from your opening statement you clearly do not agree with them did you at any point get a statement from either one of the prosecutors yes did you interview them well to be clear we did get a sworn statement which i believe is that page nine sixty of the joint appendix from gary ackley in which for among other things he never mentions the fact that he never mentions the account that he is now provided to in the amicus brief brief or the van trees family and as to ms smother men miss mother was interviewed both by the independent the legislature's independent counsel and by the attorney general's independent counsel mr duncan she gave different answers each time none of which was the account she's now provided in an unsworn letter attached to the the van trees brief it would seem that because not only you know their reputations are being impugned but they are central to this case it would seem that they an interview of these two prosecutors would be central they suggest that the their interviews were generally about prosecuting capital cases and not specifically about the details of their current unsworn statement appended at the very last minute for the very first time in a merits amica spree before this court deserves all the benefit of the doubt that they you know to which they are entitled when in the context of mr ackley did file an affidavit it is in the record in this case and it is not in any way consistent with his current accounts unavailable sorry to interrupt he made himself available he provided us a declaration no it's what you're saying is would make sense if for some reason they had made them available they suggest that they were not sought out and given an opportunity
